description: Parse unnamed generics inside (...) lists and support native trailing-error multi-return functions with automatic ? propagation.

userStories.title: Parse unnamed generics in signatures
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need unnamed generic types (Option[D], Vec[int]) to parse inside parenthesized parameter and result lists so that generic and multi-value signatures are accepted.
acceptanceCriteria: goal build transpiles `func f(x Option[int]) {}`, `func dims() (Vec[int], int) {}`, and `func p() ([]int, Option[string]) { return nil, Option.None }` with no `[syntax] expected type` error.
The fix lives in internal/parser/parser.goal (nameThenType plus an afterBracketGroup balanced-bracket scan), and internal/parser/parser.go is regenerated so verify-generated passes.
A new parser/corpus golden case exercises an unnamed generic type in BOTH param position and result position and passes.
No existing parser or corpus test regresses.
userStories.notes: Root cause: nameThenType mis-splits `IDENT [` as name+array-type. Proven fix (spike branch spike/multi-return-question): when the token after the ident-run is `[`, scan the balanced `[...]` and branch on the token after `]` — a type => array (name group), `,`/`)` => one unnamed generic type. This story is independently useful and unblocks writing US-002+ test signatures.

userStories.title: Lower trailing-error multi-return ?
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need `?` inside a native (T1, .., Tn, error) function to lower to Go that binds the leading values and propagates the error, so multi-value fallible functions transpile and run.
acceptanceCriteria: A function `func f(...) (A, B, error) { a, b := g()?; ... }` transpiles; the emitted Go compiles and, at runtime, returns zero-valued A and B plus g's error when g fails, and the computed values when g succeeds.
Bare `g()?` (discard) and single-leading `a := g()?` also lower correctly inside a native trailing-error function, and `?` on a Result[T,error] callee inside such a function lowers correctly.

Implemented in internal/backend/emit.goal (regenerated to emit.go); the rewrite engages ONLY when the function body contains a `?` site, so signatures of `?`-free error-returning functions are unchanged (no existing golden churns).
New backendtest/corpus cases cover at least five varieties including a two-leading-slot zero-fill and a struct or slice leading slot; task check is green.
userStories.notes: This is the proven spike: ~60 lines adding a nativeErr/leadNames path (nativeTrailingError detector, unwrapNative emit, funcResults named returns, bodyHasUnwrap gate, routing in stmt + unwrap). EDIT emit.goal and regenerate — do NOT hand-edit emit.go. Build path does not gate on sema, so this story's build/run tests pass before US-003 loosens the checker. Named-return zero-fill generalizes the existing `return ok,err` trick to N slots.

userStories.title: Accept multi-return ? in the checker
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need `goal check` and the LSP to accept `?` inside trailing-error multi-return functions so that build and check agree.
acceptanceCriteria: goal check reports NO question-outside-result for `func f() (int, error) { v := g()?; return v, nil }` and NO question-assign-unsupported for a matching multi-value bind `a, b := g()?` where g returns (A, B, error).
CheckQuestionOutsideResult and CheckQuestionAssignForm (internal/sema/question.goal) treat an all-unnamed result list ending in error, with no other failure-typed slot, as failure-carrying.
A sema test asserts the diagnostics are ABSENT for these shapes and STILL PRESENT for a non-fallible `func f() (int, int)` that uses `?`.
Regenerated; task check green (build and check now agree on the same multi-return inputs).
userStories.notes: Recognition should be permissive-but-safe: accept a trailing-error result only when NO non-last slot is itself error/Option/Result-typed, so the ambiguous shapes are excluded here and US-004 only adds their located diagnostics. LSP inherits the loosening via shared sema — verify no lsptest regressions.

userStories.title: Reject ambiguous multi-failure signatures
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need a clear, located error when a signature carries more than one failure-typed slot, so `?` propagation stays unambiguous.
acceptanceCriteria: goal check emits a distinct located checker code for `func f() (error, error)`, `func f() (Option[A], error)`, and `func f() (Result[A, error], error)` (two or more failure-typed slots).
The valid single-trailing-error shape `(A, B, error)` and a single whole-return Result[T,E]/Option[T] are NOT flagged.
A sema test covers each rejected shape and the accepted shapes.
Regenerated; task check green.
userStories.notes: Supersedes the previously-planned blanket 'Result mixed into a tuple' refusal (features/03-result/SYNTAX.md:152): we now allow the trailing-error tuple and refuse only the genuinely ambiguous multi-failure shapes. Rule: at most one failure-typed (error/Option/Result) slot, and it must be last.

userStories.title: Forbid discarding a trailing failure slot
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need blank-discarding the trailing error of a multi-return call to be rejected, so unignorability survives the move away from single-channel Result.
acceptanceCriteria: goal check emits a must-use diagnostic for `x, _ := f()` where f is a trailing-error multi-return (the failure slot is discarded via `_`).
Explicit propagation `x := f()?` and full binding `x, err := f()` with err used are NOT flagged.
Extends internal/sema/mustuse.goal (and internal/typecheck/mustuse.goal as needed); a sema test covers a discarded case and a used case.
The goal compiler's own source and the corpus remain green under the new rule (any newly-flagged sanctioned discards are fixed or explicitly exempted); regenerated; task check green.
userStories.notes: KEY DESIGN COST and BLAST-RADIUS RISK: this is the guarantee single-channel Result gave structurally. Extending must-use to native trailing-error tuples may newly flag existing `_`-discards across the self-hosted compiler source — assess scope first; if large, narrow the rule (e.g. only flag `_` on an error-typed trailing slot) and/or split the discard-site cleanup into a follow-up. May exceed one context window; re-split if so.

userStories.title: Support named trailing-error result lists
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need `?` to work in functions with named results like (a int, err error), reusing the declared names instead of gensyms.
acceptanceCriteria: `func f() (a int, err error) { v := g()?; return v, nil }` transpiles and runs; on failure it returns the (zero) named results plus the error, reusing the declared `err`/`a` names rather than generated ones.
goal check accepts the named-result form (no question diagnostics).
A backendtest/corpus case covers a named trailing-error result list with `?`.
Regenerated; task check green.
userStories.notes: The spike handled unnamed result lists only. If the team prefers, this can be dropped as an explicit non-goal — but it is a natural completeness gap; sequence it after the core (US-002..US-005) lands.

userStories.title: Reconcile spec with multi-return support
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the design spec and result feature docs to reflect that trailing-error multi-return with `?` is now supported, with only ambiguous multi-failure shapes refused.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/goal-design-spec.md section 3.2 no longer states a blanket refusal of a trailing-error tuple; it documents the trailing-error convention and the at-most-one-failure-slot rule.
features/03-result/SYNTAX.md shows a `(A, B, error)` trailing-error example as OK and keeps the ambiguous multi-failure / non-last examples as REJECTED.
grep confirms the old blanket 'Mixing Result into a tuple ... Refused' claim is revised, and the by-example / guide verifier in the test suite passes with the updated examples.
Regenerated (if any AI-KNOWLEDGE bootstrap doc is derived); task check green.
userStories.notes: Machine-gated via the byexample/guide verifier: any `// REJECTED` example in SYNTAX.md that now transpiles will fail that verifier until the doc is corrected, so keep doc and behavior in lockstep.

userStories.title: Fix (error,error) misconversion in goal fix
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need `goal fix` to NOT convert a (error, error) function into Result[error, error], since neither error is a success value.
acceptanceCriteria: goal fix leaves `func f() (error, error)` unchanged, reporting it out-of-scope like other multi-non-error shapes rather than rewriting it to a Result.
The classifier in internal/fix/resultsig.goal skips conversion when a non-last result is itself error-typed.
A fix golden/corpus test covers the (error, error) case (unchanged) and confirms the normal (T, error) -> Result[T, error] conversion still works.
Regenerated; task check green.
userStories.notes: Latent migration-only bug found during the spike research (resultsig.goal ~L124: gate is `len(types) > 2` + last-is-error, but never checks types[0] isn't error-like). Independent of the runtime feature; shares the 'one failure slot' intuition with US-004 — reuse a helper if natural.
